object_properties.label: belongs to organization
object_properties.description: Hierarchical relationship indicating that this organization belongs to or is part of another organization.

updated_fields.description: A formal organization entity with type, contact information, hierarchical relationships, and web presence. Represents organizations that may be hierarchically related to other organizations.
The type or category of the organization (e.g., University, Company, Government, NGO). Semantics and standardization level require confirmation.
A URI identifier for the organization. May reference external linked data resources, internal identifiers, or organization web URIs; semantics require confirmation.
Maps each row in organizations table to an Organization instance. Identity based on OrgID primary key. No row-selection condition applied; all rows represent valid organizations.

decision_summary.reason: Column Belongsto (int, nullable) is semantically distinct from descriptive attributes. Lacks explicit FK constraint but column name and type strongly suggest self-referential hierarchical relationship to organizations.OrgID. Modeled as ObjectProperty:belongsToOrganization with self-referential join path. Conservative confidence (0.7) due to lack of explicit constraint; requires schema/value probe confirmation.

No column in organizations table acts as a class-defining condition (e.g., type flag, status, publication state). Type column contains organization category/type values, not row-selection signals. All rows represent valid Organization instances. Condition remains empty.
OrgID is primary key (NOT NULL, int). Suitable for stable URI pattern identity. Template 'organizations/@@organizations.OrgID@@' is appropriate for global resource construction.

Address bundle (Address, Location, Postcode, Country) currently modeled as separate literal data properties. No dedicated Location or Address entity class detected in current scope. Conservative approach: retain as literals pending discovery of related entity tables. If Location/Address entities exist elsewhere, refactor to object properties in future revision.

URI column semantics unclear: may reference external linked data, internal identifiers, or web URIs. Retained as DataProperty:hasOrganizationURI with datatype xsd:anyURI (conservative literal modeling). Requires value sample probe to determine if refactoring to object property is justified.

Type column values unknown; unclear if standardized categories or free-text. Retained as ordinary data property. If standardized enumeration discovered, may warrant future subclass modeling or controlled vocabulary constraint.

needs_probe.question: Is there an explicit foreign key constraint on organizations.Belongsto? If so, does it reference organizations.OrgID (self-referential) or another table?
What are 3-5 sample values in the Belongsto column? Do they reference OrgID values from the same table (hierarchical parent), or do they reference a different entity type?
What are 3-5 sample values in the Type column? Are they standardized categories (e.g., 'University', 'Company', 'Government', 'NGO') or free-text descriptions?
What are 3-5 sample values in the URI column? Do they reference external linked data resources, internal identifiers, or organization web URIs?
What are 3-5 sample values in the Location column? Are they city names, venue names, region codes, or other location representations?
